## [00:00 - 03:02]

Conversations are filled with so many golden opportunities for deeper engagement that are often overlooked. These are what I call conversational threads, topics that are sprinkled all throughout every single interaction you have waiting to be unpacked. I'll give you a few examples to see if you can spot the threads. So I want to introduce you to my two volunteers. I've got Barry and Sarah. G'day, I'm Sarah. I'm Barry. Thanks guys. What we're going to do is we're going to give you three examples where there will be clear conversational threads that exist in the conversation, but neither of them pick it up. So here's example one. When did you get here? Oh, I've been here for hours mate. Just got my poison cancer, unfortunately. Spilling. Did you notice what the thread was there? There was a clear thread there, but they didn't pick it up. Here's example two. What are you going to order? I'm going to order spaghetti bolognese. My grandma used to make it all the time. Cool. I'm getting the culinary. Again, wasn't picked up. Here's example three. How's your day been? Oh yeah, day's been okay. Just a little jet lagged and my arm's still sore. Yeah, I didn't sleep all that night either. Now I'm going to make the threads more obvious for you, and then I want you to see what happens when they get picked up. Let me show you the first example again. When did you get here? Oh, I've been here for hours. My bloody appointment got cancelled. Yeah, that sucks. What was the appointment for? Oh, I just needed to look into why my jaw keeps getting detached from my face. See? Wasn't that brilliant? All of a sudden, we were able to find out more about Sarah and her jaw issue. That was fantastic. Let's see example two again where we pick up the threads. What are you going to order? Oh, I reckon I'm going to order the spaghetti bolognese mate. My grandma used to make it all the time. Sounds like your grandma are close. Tell me more about her. Yeah, grandma, she's great. She used to always put on these family dinners, get family together. But you can see in these examples, the second time round, when you pick up those threads, you're able to have more engaging conversations. Ignoring them means that you're missing out on a world of opportunities to engage on a deeper level. The first step is to really listen out for these threads.
